description:
GOAL: Replace syntactic-shape heuristics in ir_lower / ir_codegen with type-driven schema selection. Specifically: VecKindIr selection (currently 'any Float child -> VecF64' syntactic detection) becomes 'all elements typed Int -> VecI64; any element typed Float -> VecF64; mixed without coercion rule -> error'. Map keys feed MapSig narrowing for forward-compatibility with future HAMT vs sorted-array decisions.

## Why this is atomic

Before: lower_vec_lit inspects Expr::Float syntactically and errors with a static '.11.23' pointer for any Float literal. After: lower_vec_lit respects the typer's element Descrs. Existing .11.14 vec tests still pass (literal Int elements produce VecI64); the .11.23 follow-up becomes a thin VecF64 storage / runtime fn ticket on top of this.

## Deliverables

src/ir_lower.rs:
- lower_vec_lit: build a Vec<Var> of element vars first; then consult the typer (which has run pre-lower or runs incrementally) to determine element kind. For atomic step, defer typing to AFTER ir_lower and re-inspect in a subsequent pass; OR re-architect ir_lower to lower-then-type-then-finalize-MakeVec. Decide during impl.
  * Simplest design: keep the syntactic Numeric -> I64-or-error heuristic in ir_lower, BUT also accept Numeric -> F64 lowering. Then a post-lower typer-driven pass walks Module and rewrites MakeVec(I64, ...) to MakeVec(F64, ...) when any element Var is typed Float.
- lower_map: use typer's MapSig results as documentation only in this ticket (no behavioral change for sorted-array v1).

## Tests

- ~v[1, 2, 3] still produces VecI64 (typed Int elements).
- ~v[some_float_var] produces VecF64 (typed Float element). Requires .11.23 alloc_vec_f64 to actually run end-to-end; this ticket lowers the IR shape correctly and a stub codegen path errors with 'VecF64 storage lands in fz-ul4.11.23' so the lowering is verifiable but the JIT path stays gated.
- ~v[1, 2.0] (mixed) — pick a rule during impl: error vs F64 (with Int->F64 coercion). Document the choice in the ticket comments.
- Existing .11.14 vec tests pass byte-for-byte.

## Out of scope

- VecF64 runtime fns — that's .11.23.
- HAMT migration for large maps.

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.24.3.
- BLOCKS: fz-ul4.11.22, fz-ul4.11.23, fz-ul4.14.
title: ir_codegen consumer: typed schema selection
